title: Who You Are in Christ — Part 2
intro: Yesterday we looked at what has been removed — condemnation, guilt, the record of sin. Today we look at what has been given. Because the gospel is not only about what God took away. It is about what He poured in.
reading:
Gavin's outline continues the identity section with three more aspects of who you are in Christ — plus what you have been given positionally.

You are a temple of the Holy Spirit. 1 Corinthians 6:19-20 declares that your body is the dwelling place of the living God. You are not empty. You are not abandoned. You carry the presence of God.

You are a citizen of heaven. Philippians 3:20 says our citizenship is in heaven. Ephesians 2:19 tells us we are no longer strangers and aliens but fellow citizens with the saints. You belong to an eternal kingdom that cannot be shaken.

You are accepted in the Beloved. Ephesians 1:6 declares He has made us accepted in the Beloved — not tolerated, not conditionally received, but accepted fully and freely in Christ.

You are blessed with every spiritual blessing. Ephesians 1:3 declares that God has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places. Not some blessings. Every spiritual blessing — already given, already yours.

You are seated with Christ in heavenly places. Ephesians 2:6 says God raised us up with Him and seated us with Him. Your position in the spiritual realm is not on the floor. You reign with Christ.

You are complete in Him. Colossians 2:10 says you have been filled in Him who is the head of all rule and authority. In Christ, you lack nothing.
reflection: Of all these truths — temple, citizen, accepted, blessed, seated, complete — which one feels most foreign to the way you actually live day to day? What would change if you genuinely believed it?
abide: Lord, I am not lacking. I am not second-class. I am blessed with every spiritual blessing, seated with Christ, accepted and complete in Him. Let me live from that fullness today instead of begging for what You have already given.
